exemplar.title: Prompt 13: How can we know that current knowledge is an improvement upon past knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: Prompt 13: How can we know that current knowledge is an improvement upon past knowledge?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific, historically grounded objects
Coherent chronological structure linking artefacts to technological progression
Explicit, broadly coherent links between each object and the chosen IA prompt
Use of relevant factual details (e.g., time spans) and a comprehensive reference list
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive with limited engagement in epistemic concepts
Introduction and conclusion contain unsupported assertions and some repetition
Occasional vagueness in justifying links (e.g., glossed transitions between technologies)
Inconsistent citation detail and some reliance on sources of questionable authority
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets all the descriptors for the top of the 5â6 band: it clearly presents three well-contextualized objects, explains how each illustrates progression of knowledge, and provides a defensible justification for their inclusion. The links to the IA prompt are explicit and supported by factual details, demonstrating a good level of research and organization. However, the commentary lacks depth in epistemic analysis, contains unsupported or broad assertions in places, and could improve academic rigor through precise citations and stronger engagement with TOK concepts. These limitations prevent advancement into the 7â8 band, making a final score of 6 appropriate.

annotations.comment: The introduction is overly broad and lacks a clear, operational definition of âimprovement.â To strengthen it, the student should specify criteria by which past and current knowledge are compared (e.g., reliability, efficiency, explanatory power).

The chosen AOK âKnowledge and technologyâ is appropriate, but the student should articulate how epistemic concepts (e.g., validation methods, certainty) intersect with technological change to tie it more firmly to TOK theory.
The session and word count information are administrative; these should not appear in the body of the exhibition. Focus comments on content analysis and remove formatting details.

The opening paragraph is highly descriptive and contains unsupported assertions (e.g., âever-lasting hunger for improvementâ). Introduce evidence or a brief TOK framework to ground claims about knowledge progression.

The image for Object 1 lacks a caption explaining its provenance and relevance. Always include context (source, date, location) to justify its selection and connect physically to the real-world artefact.

The section header âObject 1: Internal Combustion Engineâ clearly identifies the artefact. However, specify the exact model or serial number to demonstrate a specific real-world context rather than a generic type.

The analysis repeats phrases (âknowledge is evolvingâ). To deepen the justification, contrast specific performance metrics (e.g., thermal efficiency of steam vs. combustion engines) rather than restating the general idea.

In discussing petroleum refining, the student asserts it âplayed a pivotal roleâ but doesnât cite specific evidence (e.g., date of first refinery output). Adding a concrete fact or data point would strengthen the link.
The Wright Flyer image needs a citation and caption to show its specific location or archive. Without this, the artefact remains abstract rather than grounded in a real-world context.

The claim that lunar landing technologies directly stem from the Wright Flyer is too simplistic. Acknowledge intermediary milestones (e.g., jet propulsion, aerodynamics studies) to present a more nuanced progression of knowledge.

The transition from the Wright Flyerâs 12-hp engine to Saturn V propulsion is glossed. Integrate specific scientific theories (e.g., Newtonâs laws) and explain how each advanced understanding applied to rocket design.
The caption for the Saturn V image is missing. To ground the artefact, include launch date, location, and specific mission number, reinforcing the real-world context required for full marks.

Edisonâs lightbulb discussion notes Franklinâs contributions but lacks analysis of epistemic validation (e.g., testing of filaments). Explore how experimental methods improved certainty in electrical theories.

The concluding paragraph reiterates earlier points without adding new insight. To avoid repetition, synthesize how these three objects collectively illustrate epistemic growth in knowledge production mechanisms.
References are generally appropriate but several URLs lack access dates and proper formatting. Follow a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) to enhance credibility and academic rigor.
Several online sources (e.g., weebly.com, bulbs.com) are of questionable authority. The student should prioritize peer-reviewed or primary historical research to substantiate technological claims.
The reference list omits in-text citations. Ensure each factual claim in the body is directly linked to a corresponding entry, improving traceability and evidence support.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and contexts, meeting level 5â6 requirements. To reach level 7â8, the student should deepen conceptual analysis and make explicit references to the IA prompt in each section.

Strong selection of tangible historical artefacts (engine, flyer, bulb) demonstrates how technological knowledge evolves. These concrete objects help ground the abstract TOK question in the real world.
Each object is situated in a clear historical timeline, aiding coherence across the exhibition. This chronological structure effectively supports the overarching prompt about knowledge improvement.
Use of quantitative time span (â66 yearsâ) between first flight and lunar landing provides a compelling metric to illustrate the rate of knowledge improvement.
The argument that Edisonâs lightbulb built directly on Franklinâs work is well-founded and shows the studentâs understanding of knowledge chains across time.
The reference list is comprehensive, covering engines, aviation, and lighting history. This breadth demonstrates good research practice and supports the exhibitionâs factual claims.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?

three objects, but the
real-world contexts of

highly generic images of

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
